title: Add apt-get update before apt package installation
description: pmf-init.sh attempts apt-get install without prior apt-get update, causing 'Unable to locate package' errors on fresh Ubuntu systems. Add automatic 'sudo apt-get update -y' before any apt-get install call. Only run once per script execution, cache the update state.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN pmf-init.sh runs on a fresh Ubuntu with no apt cache THEN apt-get update runs automatically before first apt install; WHEN apt-get update already ran in this session THEN it is not repeated; WHEN running with sudo THEN -E flag preserves proxy environment variables; ref: pmf-init.sh:372
review_initial_requirements: Verify apt-get update is called exactly once before first apt install; verify -E flag on sudo; verify update state is cached to avoid repeated calls
review_regression_requirements: Verify apt-get update still runs on fresh systems; verify no regression on systems with existing cache
notes: source_doc:docs/testing/2026-06-14-real-machine/test-report.md; bug_ref:BUG-01; picked_reason:P0 first issue; done_at:2026-06-14; evidence:ensure_apt_updated() with APT_UPDATED cache + sudo -E on all apt commands; validation_limited:MANUAL test_mcp - cannot run apt-get install in agent env; manual_test:Run ./pmf-init.sh --yes on fresh Ubuntu without node/npm to verify apt-get update runs once before install

title: Add sudo to npm global install commands for non-root users
description: npm install -g fails with EACCES on non-root users because /usr/local/lib/node_modules is root-owned. Detect whether current user has write access to npm global prefix; if not, prepend sudo to npm install -g commands. Also handle nvm-managed node where sudo is not needed.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN user is non-root and npm global prefix is /usr/local THEN sudo is prepended to npm install -g; WHEN user is root THEN no sudo is added; WHEN node is managed by nvm (prefix in $HOME) THEN no sudo is added; WHEN sudo is not available THEN error message suggests using nvm or running as root; ref: pmf-init.sh:372
review_initial_requirements: Verify non-root user can install npm global packages; verify nvm detection works; verify root user path unchanged; verify error message when sudo unavailable
review_regression_requirements: Verify npm install commands still work for root, non-root, and nvm users after changes
notes: source_doc:docs/testing/2026-06-14-real-machine/test-report.md; bug_ref:BUG-02; picked_reason:P0 second issue; done_at:2026-06-14; evidence:npm_global_cmd_prefix() detects root/nvm/$HOME/write-access/sudo-available with fallback message; validation_limited:MANUAL test_mcp - cannot test real npm global install in agent env; manual_test:Run ./pmf-init.sh as non-root user with system npm to verify sudo is prepended; test with nvm to verify no sudo

title: Fix agy npm package name or provide alternative install method
description: The package @google/agy does not exist on npm public registry (404 error). Research the correct package name for Google Antigravity CLI. If no public npm package exists, provide alternative installation: check if agy binary already exists, or install via other means (go install, direct binary download, etc). Update detect_tool and install_tool accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN agy install is triggered THEN the correct package name or install method is used; WHEN @google/agy does not exist on npm THEN fallback install method is attempted; WHEN no install method works THEN a clear message explains how to install agy manually with a URL; ref: pmf-init.sh:374
review_initial_requirements: Verify the install command actually succeeds on a clean system; verify detect_tool still correctly detects agy --version; verify fallback message is helpful
review_regression_requirements: Verify agy detection and installation still work after package name change
notes: source_doc:docs/testing/2026-06-14-real-machine/test-report.md; bug_ref:BUG-03; picked_reason:P1 next after P0s; done_at:2026-06-14; evidence:agy install changed from npm to curl installer (https://antigravity.google/cli/install.sh); fallback message with manual URL on failure; tier4_check_cmds updated; validation_limited:MANUAL - cannot run agy installer in agent env; manual_test:Run ./pmf-init.sh and trigger agy install to verify curl installer is used

title: Add API Key pre-check before graphify project initialization
description: When graphify . runs without LLM API keys (GEMINI_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, etc), it fails 3 times with retry delays totaling ~20s. Add pre-check: if no API key env vars are set, skip graphify . with a clear message explaining which keys are needed. In --yes mode, auto-skip. In interactive mode, warn and ask.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN no LLM API key env vars are set THEN graphify . is skipped with message listing required keys (GEMINI_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY); WHEN at least one API key is set THEN graphify . runs normally with retry; WHEN --yes mode and no keys THEN auto-skip without prompt; ref: pmf-init.sh:259
review_initial_requirements: Verify API key detection covers GEMINI_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; verify skip message is clear; verify graphify . still works when keys are present
review_regression_requirements: Verify API key check does not break normal graphify flow; verify no false positives on key detection

title: Unify skip log format for report parser compatibility
description: Phase skip messages use inconsistent formats across phases. Standardize all skip outputs to use a single format: '⏭️ [component]: 用户跳过 [reason]' so the Phase 7 report generator can reliably parse and list all skipped items.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN user skips any item via 'n' THEN log output matches format '⏭️ [component]: 用户跳过'; WHEN user skips entire phase via 's' THEN all components in that phase are logged with skip format; WHEN Phase 7 parses skip entries THEN all skipped items appear in the report's '用户跳过项' section with consistent formatting; ref: pmf-init.sh:549
review_initial_requirements: Verify skip format is consistent across all 7 phases; verify Phase 7 report parser correctly captures all skipped items
review_regression_requirements: Verify skip format changes don't break existing report generation; verify both 'n' and 's' produce parseable output

title: Review documented vision against delivered work
description: Use a same-model sub-agent review to compare approved-doc claims with delivered behavior, evidence level, CSV state, validation evidence, and review log.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all non-review issues before this row are closed THEN run same-model sub-agent review against source-specific claim/evidence checks; WHEN gaps or overstated claims are found THEN append follow-up issues and REVIEW-02; WHEN no gaps remain THEN close the CSV.
review_initial_requirements: Verify all prior non-review rows are closed before running this review.
review_regression_requirements: Run same-model sub-agent review against approved doc goals, non-goals, acceptance criteria, delivered diff, validation evidence, prior review logs, and source-specific claim/evidence alignment checks. Task-specific checks: (1) BUG-01 fix: apt-get update runs exactly once and -E preserves proxy vars on real fresh Ubuntu; (2) BUG-02 fix: npm global install succeeds for non-root without nvm AND for nvm users without sudo; (3) BUG-03 fix: agy installs successfully or provides actionable fallback, not a silent skip; (4) BUG-04 fix: graphify . is skipped when no API key present, not just warned then retried
